steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: A planet closer to the Sun orbits…
options: More slowly, because it has less distance to travel
At the same speed as all other planets
Faster, because gravity is stronger at smaller orbital radii
Faster only if it is more massive than the outer planets
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Closer orbits mean stronger gravity, so the planet must travel faster to maintain its orbit. A smaller orbital radius also means a shorter circumference, so the orbital period is much shorter.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! The closer a planet is to the Sun, the stronger the gravitational pull and the faster it must travel to stay in orbit. It also has a shorter orbital period.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.8.1 — Orbital motion

steps.title: Orbital Motion
steps.bodyHTML: Gravity provides the centripetal force that keeps planets and moons in orbit. For a circular orbit, the gravitational force acts towards the centre, constantly changing the direction of motion without doing work on the object. A planet in a smaller orbital radius experiences a stronger gravitational pull and must travel at a greater orbital speed to maintain its orbit, resulting in a shorter orbital period.
commonErrors: Thinking gravity does work on an object in circular orbit — in a perfect circle the force is perpendicular to motion so no work is done
Forgetting that closer orbits are faster AND have shorter periods
Confusing orbital speed with orbital period

steps.title: See it in action
steps.instruction: Drag the slider to change the orbital radius. Watch how the relative orbital period changes — smaller radius means shorter period.

aqa.questionText: Venus is closer to the Sun than Earth. Explain why Venus has a shorter orbital period than Earth.

steps.label: State the effect of distance on gravity
steps.content: Venus is closer to the Sun, so the gravitational force on Venus is stronger than on Earth.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — stronger gravitational force at smaller distance

steps.label: Explain orbital speed
steps.content: To stay in orbit against this stronger gravity, Venus must travel at a higher orbital speed.

steps.label: Explain shorter period
steps.content: Venus also has a smaller orbit to travel around, so it completes one full orbit in a shorter time — a shorter orbital period.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — smaller orbit circumference so shorter period

aqa.finalAnswer: Stronger gravity at smaller radius → higher orbital speed + shorter circumference → shorter period.

aqa.questionText: Which of the following statements about planetary orbits is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: All planets orbit the Sun at the same speed

options.label: B
options.text: A planet further from the Sun has a shorter orbital period

options.label: C
options.text: The orbital period increases as the orbital radius increases

options.label: D
options.text: The gravitational force on a planet does not depend on its distance from the Sun

markSchemeSteps: Larger orbital radius → weaker gravity → lower speed and larger orbit → longer period ✓
aqa.commonMistake: Option B is the reverse of the correct relationship — further planets have LONGER orbital periods.
